layout.structure: Dashboard-first with fixed left sidebar navigation

sidebar.style: fixed left-0 top-0 h-full border-r border-slate-200 bg-white z-30 hidden md:flex flex-col

cards.style: bg-white rounded-xl border border-slate-200 shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-200
cards.header: flex flex-row items-center justify-between space-y-0 pb-2

buttons.primary: bg-slate-900 text-white hover:bg-slate-800 shadow-sm rounded-lg px-4 py-2 font-medium transition-all active:scale-95
buttons.secondary: bg-white text-slate-900 border border-slate-200 hover:bg-slate-50 shadow-sm rounded-lg px-4 py-2 font-medium transition-all active:scale-95
buttons.ghost: hover:bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 transition-colors

inputs.style: flex h-10 w-full rounded-lg border border-slate-200 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm ring-offset-white file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-slate-500 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-500 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50

charts.style: Clean, minimal grid lines, custom tooltips with rounded corners and shadow

profile_wife.description: Professional woman in floral shirt, smiling

goal_house.description: Modern house exterior with wooden facade

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement the 'Monarch-style' aesthetic: generous whitespace, soft shadows, and rounded corners (12-16px).
Use the 'Bento Grid' layout for the Dashboard: mix large summary cards with smaller metric cards.
Ensure the Sidebar is fixed on the left and the main content area scrolls independently.
Use 'Outfit' font for all headings to give it a modern, friendly character. Use 'Inter' for body text.
Implement the 'Blue Accent' (#3B82F6) sparingly for active states, primary buttons, and key data points.
Use Shadcn UI components for all interactive elements (Dialogs, Sheets, Dropdowns, Forms).
Customize Shadcn components to match the 'rounded-xl' and 'shadow-sm' aesthetic.
Use Recharts for all data visualization. Ensure charts are clean with minimal grid lines.
Implement the 'Me' and 'Wife' household member logic with distinct color coding (e.g., Blue for Me, Pink/Purple for Wife) in avatars and transaction tags.
Ensure all buttons and tabs are functional as per the requirements.
Add 'data-testid' to all interactive elements for testing.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add '.App { text-align: center; }' in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed.
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones.
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...).
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...}).
Use 'sonner' for toasts. Sonner component are located in '/app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx'.
